game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Download Friend's Pass* and play It Takes Two with a friend for free. Just grab a friend who owns the full game, download Friend's Pass for free, and play together! Download Friend's Pass* to play It Takes Two with a friend — for free!

Friend's Pass lets you play It Takes Two with a friend, provided they already own the full game.

Here's how it works:
  • Grab a friend who owns the full game of It Takes Two.
  • Download Friend's Pass for free.
  • Play the entire game together!

    Because whether it's splitting an ice cream sundae, grabbing a drink, spending the night on the couch or going on the craziest video game adventure of your life — everything's better when we do it together.
game_info.description: Two friends are trapped in a magical realm and must work together to escape! Control both characters simultaneously - one with arrow keys, one with other keys. Navigate through increasingly challenging platforming levels, collect keys to unlock doors, avoid deadly spikes, and reach the exit together. Both friends must survive and reach the goal to succeed. Cooperation is key!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move Player 1 (Blue) - Left/Right to move, Up to jump
WASD Keys: Move Player 2 (Red) - A/D to move, W to jump
Space: Switch active control between players (for single-player mode)
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (return to menu)
ENTER: Start game from menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and collision testing. Validates that both players can move, jump, and interact with platforms correctly. Tests basic physics like gravity and collision detection with the environment.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move both players around the starting area, test jumping mechanics, verify collision with platforms and walls. Ensure players don't fall through floors or pass through walls.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Both players should move smoothly, jump predictably, and collide properly with all surfaces. No clipping through geometry. Players should respond to gravity consistently.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition testing - optimal strategy to complete the game. Tests pathfinding, key collection, door unlocking, hazard avoidance, and cooperative goal reaching.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate both players through the level efficiently, collect all required keys, unlock doors, avoid spike hazards, and guide both players to the exit portal. Use optimal jumping and movement patterns.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Both players successfully collect keys, unlock doors, avoid all hazards, and reach the exit together, triggering GAME_OVER_WIN state within reasonable time.

TEST_3.test_description: Hazard interaction testing. Validates that touching spikes causes game over and tests the death/respawn mechanics.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately move one player into spike hazards to trigger death condition. Verify that game over state is reached correctly.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE when either player touches spikes. Proper game over screen should display with restart option.

TEST_4.test_description: Key and door mechanics testing. Validates collection system and door unlocking progression.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Guide players to collect keys in sequence and verify doors unlock properly. Test that locked doors block passage and unlocked doors allow passage.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Keys should be collectible, doors should unlock when keys are collected, and progression should be gated properly until requirements are met.
